I am trying to download goes xrs data following [this tutorial](https://docs.sunpy.org/en/stable/generated/gallery/time_series/goes_xrs_example.html). The following command
```python
result_goes15 = Fido.search(a.Time(tstart, tend), a.Instrument("XRS"), a.goes.SatelliteNumber(15), a.Resolution("avg1m"))
```
ran for over 12 minutes. I interrupted it and find it was stuck at
```python
File /opt/anaconda/anaconda3/envs/py12/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sunpy/net/scraper.py:285, in Scraper.filelist(self, timerange)
    [283](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/opt/anaconda/anaconda3/envs/py12/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sunpy/net/scraper.py:283) directory = directories.pop(0)
    [284](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/opt/anaconda/anaconda3/envs/py12/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sunpy/net/scraper.py:284) try:
--> [285](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/opt/anaconda/anaconda3/envs/py12/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sunpy/net/scraper.py:285)     opn = urlopen(directory)
    [286](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/opt/anaconda/anaconda3/envs/py12/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sunpy/net/scraper.py:286)     try:
    [287](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/opt/anaconda/anaconda3/envs/py12/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sunpy/net/scraper.py:287)         soup = BeautifulSoup(opn, "html.parser")
```

I waited longer and got an exception `URLError: <urlopen error [Errno 101] Network is unreachable>`. The full stacktrace is as follows:

### To Reproduce

from sunpy import timeseries as ts
from sunpy.net import Fido
from sunpy.net import attrs as a

result_goes15 = Fido.search(a.Time(tstart, tend), a.Instrument("XRS"), a.goes.SatelliteNumber(15), a.Resolution("flx1s"))
